source_file: The corporate social credit system in China and its transnational impact.txt
text:
87
   See European Union Chamber of Commerce in China (n 13) 12–13.
88
   See Carlson and Hwang (n 81). Attaching responsibility to corporations for the behaviour of their
   business partner and companies in their supply chain is also attempted by US-American, Australian
   and European laws, eg the Loi de vigilance (Légifrance, LOI n° 2017-399 du 27 mars 2017 relative
   au devoir de vigilance des sociétés mères et des entreprises donneuses d’ordre, online: www.
   legifrance.gouv.fr/aﬃchTexte.do?cidTexte=JORFTEXT000034290626&categorieLien=id), the British
   Modern Slavery Act (The National Archives, Modern Slavery Act, 2015, online: www.legislation.gov.
   uk/ukpga/2015/30/contents/enacted) and the Lieferkettengesetz (supply chain law) adopted by the
   German Bundestag in June 2021.
89
   Friederike Böge, ‘Zensurvorgaben global umgesetzt. Zoom beugt sich China’ Frankfurter Allgemeine
   Zeitung (12 June 2020), online: www.faz.net/aktuell/politik/ausland/zoom-setzt-chinesische-
   zensurvorgaben-global-um-16812270.html.
90
   Zoom, ‘Improving Our Policies as We Continue to Enable Global Collaboration’ (Zoom Blog, 11 June
   2020), online: https://blog.zoom.us/wordpress/2020/06/11/improving-our-policies-as-we-continue-
   to-enable-global-collaboration/.
91
   See Jiang and Fu (n 30). 384.
92
   See Hoﬀman (n 11) 4.
                                                         TRANSNATIONAL LEGAL THEORY                307
